attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Other specified disorders involving the immune mechanism, not elsewhere classified

transform_flags: Age (~38) inferred — not stated in source; body weight 62 kg and typical MCAS demographic used. Confirm on clinician review.
Sex at birth (female) inferred — not stated in source (MCAS is more common in women). Confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-GI-03-00228 — SEQ derived from source CFE-028; ingest --reseq will assign the real global sequence within the SPEC-GI-03 bucket.

difficulty_tier set to red_herring_laden (03): the 'acute surgical abdomen' emergency framing is the red herring over a benign known chronic flare. diagnosis_category set to zebra_rare (MCAS is uncommon) — reviewer may prefer important_not_to_miss given the surgical mimics; flagged for confirmation.

TIER CALIBRATION (over-triage-resistance case): correct_baseline_tier T2 with prominent mandatory T4/T5 deterioration safety-netting, per the CFE complex-chronic-condition calibration — the source's own acute red-flag screen is negative and its disposition is routine (GP review 3 days). Over-triage to baseline T4/T5 is the scored defect. Reviewer to confirm T2 vs a T3 same-day uplift if oral fluids cannot be maintained.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source clinician-administered IM hyoscine butylbromide 20 mg and sublingual ondansetron 4 mg reprojected to AI-appropriate actions (recognise + oral rehydration + arrange review + avoid trigger drugs + safety-net). Source doses/routes recorded verbatim in node 12; no doses synthesised. H1/H2 antihistamine and cromoglicate doses intentionally omitted (not specified in source).

All SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes are UNVERIFIED CANDIDATES pending terminology-server receipts; MCAS (782718007), mesenteric ischaemia (129123006) and the ICD-10-AM D89.4 mapping in particular require confirmation. No AMT medication codes assigned (low confidence) — left for terminology lookup rather than guessed.

Clinician-only exam findings (soft abdomen / no guarding / no rebound / negative Murphy's & Rovsing's, hyperactive bowel sounds, tympanic distension) and measured vitals sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; patient-obtainable data (visible distension, flushing, subjective palpitations, self-reported weight) placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
